Title: Genome-wide association identifies a missing hydrolase for tocopherol synthesis in plants
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed across seed development; not expressed in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in developing seeds/green siliques; absent or very low in leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Total tocopherols (especially γ-tocopherol-rich seed vitamin E pool)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds; ectopic effects demonstrated in leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lipid-soluble antioxidant; protects membrane lipids during seed desiccation, storage and germination; essential dietary vitamin E for humans
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tocopherol accumulation; loss reduces seed total tocopherol about 54–55%, while overexpression restores mutant seed phenotype and elevates leaf total tocopherols up to 3.6-fold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified AT5G39220 as the strongest locus for Arabidopsis seed γ-tocopherol and total tocopherol variation. 2. Null Atvte7 mutants reduced seed total tocopherols by about 54–55% without affecting leaf tocopherols under native expression. 3. 35S-driven AtVTE7 complemented the mutant and ectopically increased leaf total tocopherols up to 3.6-fold. 4. VTE7 is a chloroplast-envelope alpha/beta hydrolase/esterase linked to chlorophyll biosynthetic intermediates with partially reduced prenyl side chains, supporting a role in supplying prenyl alcohols for tocopherol synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; T-DNA knockout and leaky alleles; RT-qPCR; transgenic overexpression/complementation; YFP localization; recombinant esterase assay; targeted and untargeted LC-MS metabolite profiling; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GGR; POR; CHS; LIL3 (proposed functional association, not directly validated)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using 1001 Genomes resequencing panel; RNA-seq (NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA767331); untargeted and targeted LC-MS metabolomics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages (9, 14, 18, 24 DAP); constitutive ectopic expression in leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: AtVTE7 overexpression lines were generally phenotypically similar to WT; no major chlorophyll bulk changes reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering higher vitamin E by increasing prenyl-alcohol supply to tocopherol biosynthesis; can be stacked with VTE2 for additive leaf tocopherol enhancement; likely useful in seed vitamin E improvement if expression is targeted to appropriate tissues/developmental stages
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro esterase activity was detected on synthetic p-nitrophenyl esters but not on chlorophylls, pheophytins or phytyl esters; authors propose VTE7 acts on chlorophyll biosynthetic intermediates in vivo rather than bulk chlorophyll degradation products.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73; W22 mutant background analyzed for zmvte7-1
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; chloroplast envelope inferred, with chloroplast targeting demonstrated using cTP-ZmVTE7:YFP in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strong across seed development, highest in developing embryos; also expressed in vegetative tissues/leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In maize kernels and leaves; when ectopically expressed in Arabidopsis, active in leaves and seeds of Atvte7-1 background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lipid-soluble antioxidant and human vitamin E nutrient
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tocopherol accumulation; leaky zmvte7-1 reduces kernel total tocopherols about 30–38% and leaf total tocopherols about 45–49%; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis raises leaf total tocopherols up to 6.9-fold and partially complements Atvte7 seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmVTE7 is the maize ortholog of AtVTE7 and lies in a major maize kernel total tocopherol QTL. 2. A leaky upstream Mu insertion reduced ZmVTE7 transcript by about 80% and lowered kernel and leaf tocopherols. 3. Ectopic cTP-ZmVTE7 expression in Arabidopsis Atvte7-1 increased leaf tocopherols more strongly than AtVTE7 and partially complemented seed tocopherols. 4. B73 carries tandem duplicated ZmVTE7 copies, likely contributing to superior tocopherol allele effects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mu insertion mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; tocopherol/tocotrienol profiling; transgenic complementation and overexpression in Arabidopsis; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GGR; POR; CHS; LIL3 (proposed functional association, not directly validated)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: NAM QTL/contextual association analysis; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; cTP fusion for Arabidopsis expression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kernel development; leaf tissue; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: About half of Arabidopsis cTP-ZmVTE7 overexpression lines showed anomalous leaf expansion while still elevating leaf tocopherols
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for maize vitamin E improvement; favorable copy number or expression alleles may enhance kernel tocopherol; promising transgene/editing target but dosage and leaf-development side effects should be monitored
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: B73 uniquely has two tandem ZmVTE7 copies; tocotrienol levels in kernels were unchanged in zmvte7-1, supporting a specific role in tocopherol rather than tocotrienol synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses homogentisic acid and phytyl-diphosphate to initiate tocopherol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the first committed intermediate leading to tocopherols
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/committed-step enzyme for total tocopherol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases total tocopherols up to 4.4-fold in leaves and about 40% in seeds; additive with AtVTE7 in leaves but not in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtVTE2 is the committed-step enzyme in tocopherol biosynthesis. 2. Constitutive overexpression substantially increased leaf and seed total tocopherols. 3. Combined overexpression with AtVTE7 caused additive increases in leaves, indicating substrate supply and committed-step activity can jointly elevate flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously characterized overexpression; F1 double-overexpression analysis with AtVTE7; tocopherol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtVTE7 (functional flux interaction via cosubstrate supply)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaves and seeds under constitutive overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target; stacking with VTE7 can further boost leaf vitamin E; seed effects may be constrained by other factors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimental gene because the paper directly tests AtVTE2 × AtVTE7 combinatorial overexpression for tocopherol enhancement.
